heading: Qualified business income
label: 199A Qualified business income
notes: Inflation Adjusted Items for Certain YearsFor inflation adjustment of certain items in this section, see Revenue Procedures listed in a table under .section 1 of this title

children.text: the combined qualified business income amount of the taxpayer, or

children.label: A
children.text: the taxable income of the taxpayer for the taxable year, over

children.label: B
children.text: the net capital gain (as defined in section 1(h)) of the taxpayer for such taxable year.

children.heading: Combined qualified business income amount
children.label: b Combined qualified business income amount
A
children.text: the sum of the amounts determined under paragraph (2) for each qualified trade or business carried on by the taxpayer, plus

children.label: B
children.text: 20 percent of the aggregate amount of the qualified REIT dividends and qualified publicly traded partnership income of the taxpayer for the taxable year.

children.heading: Determination of deductible amount for each trade or business
children.label: 2 Determination of deductible amount for each trade or business
A
children.text: 20 percent of the taxpayer’s qualified business income with respect to the qualified trade or business, or

children.label: i
children.text: 50 percent of the W–2 wages with respect to the qualified trade or business, or

children.label: ii
children.text: the sum of 25 percent of the W–2 wages with respect to the qualified trade or business, plus 2.5 percent of the unadjusted basis immediately after acquisition of all qualified property.

children.heading: Modifications to limit based on taxable income
children.label: 3 Modifications to limit based on taxable income

children.heading: Exception from limit
children.label: A Exception from limit
children.text: In the case of any taxpayer whose taxable income for the taxable year does not exceed the threshold amount, paragraph (2) shall be applied without regard to subparagraph (B).

children.heading: Phase-in of limit for certain taxpayers
children.label: B Phase-in of limit for certain taxpayers
I
children.text: the taxable income of a taxpayer for any taxable year exceeds the threshold amount, but does not exceed the sum of the threshold amount plus $75,000 ($150,000 in the case of a joint return), and

children.label: II
children.text: the amount determined under paragraph (2)(B) (determined without regard to this subparagraph) with respect to any qualified trade or business carried on by the taxpayer is less than the amount determined under paragraph (2)(A) with respect such trade or business,

children.label: I
children.text: the amount by which the taxpayer’s taxable income for the taxable year exceeds the threshold amount, bears to

children.label: II
children.text: $75,000 ($150,000 in the case of a joint return).

children.label: I
children.text: the amount determined under paragraph (2)(A) (determined without regard to this paragraph), over

children.label: II
children.text: the amount determined under paragraph (2)(B) (determined without regard to this paragraph).

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The term “W–2 wages” means, with respect to any person for any taxable year of such person, the amounts described in paragraphs (3) and (8) of section 6051(a) paid by such person with respect to employment of employees by such person during the calendar year ending during such taxable year.

children.heading: Limitation to wages attributable to qualified business income
children.label: B Limitation to wages attributable to qualified business income
children.text: Such term shall not include any amount which is not properly allocable to qualified business income for purposes of subsection (c)(1).

children.heading: Return requirement
children.label: C Return requirement
children.text: Such term shall not include any amount which is not properly included in a return filed with the Social Security Administration on or before the 60th day after the due date (including extensions) for such return.

children.heading: Acquisitions, dispositions, and short taxable years
children.label: 5 Acquisitions, dispositions, and short taxable years
children.text: The Secretary shall provide for the application of this subsection in cases of a short taxable year or where the taxpayer acquires, or disposes of, the major portion of a trade or business or the major portion of a separate unit of a trade or business during the taxable year.

children.label: i
children.text: which is held by, and available for use in, the qualified trade or business at the close of the taxable year,

children.label: ii
children.text: which is used at any point during the taxable year in the production of qualified business income, and

children.label: iii
children.text: the depreciable period for which has not ended before the close of the taxable year.

children.label: i
children.text: the date that is 10 years after such date, or

children.label: ii
children.text: the last day of the last full year in the applicable recovery period that would apply to the property under section 168 (determined without regard to subsection (g) thereof).

children.heading: Special rule with respect to income received from cooperatives
children.label: 7 Special rule with respect to income received from cooperatives
A
children.text: 9 percent of so much of the qualified business income with respect to such trade or business as is properly allocable to qualified payments received from such cooperative, or

children.label: B
children.text: 50 percent of so much of the W–2 wages with respect to such trade or business as are so allocable.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: The term “qualified business income” means, for any taxable year, the net amount of qualified items of income, gain, deduction, and loss with respect to any qualified trade or business of the taxpayer. Such term shall not include any qualified REIT dividends or qualified publicly traded partnership income.

children.heading: Carryover of losses
children.label: 2 Carryover of losses
children.text: If the net amount of qualified income, gain, deduction, and loss with respect to qualified trades or businesses of the taxpayer for any taxable year is less than zero, such amount shall be treated as a loss from a qualified trade or business in the succeeding taxable year.

children.heading: Qualified items of income, gain, deduction, and loss
children.label: 3 Qualified items of income, gain, deduction, and loss
i
children.text: effectively connected with the conduct of a trade or business within the United States (within the meaning of section 864(c), determined by substituting “qualified trade or business (within the meaning of section 199A)” for “nonresident alien individual or a foreign corporation” or for “a foreign corporation” each place it appears), and1So in original. The word “a” probably should not appear within the quoted text.1

children.label: ii
children.text: included or allowed in determining taxable income for the taxable year.

children.label: i
children.text: Any item of short-term capital gain, short-term capital loss, long-term capital gain, or long-term capital loss.

children.label: ii
children.text: Any dividend, income equivalent to a dividend, or payment in lieu of dividends described in section 954(c)(1)(G). Any amount described in section 1385(a)(1) shall not be treated as described in this clause.

children.label: iii
children.text: Any interest income other than interest income which is properly allocable to a trade or business.

children.label: iv
children.text: Any item of gain or loss described in subparagraph (C) or (D) of section 954(c)(1) (applied by substituting “qualified trade or business” for “controlled foreign corporation”).

children.label: v
children.text: Any item of income, gain, deduction, or loss taken into account under section 954(c)(1)(F) (determined without regard to clause (ii) thereof and other than items attributable to notional principal contracts entered into in transactions qualifying under section 1221(a)(7)).

children.label: vi
children.text: Any amount received from an annuity which is not received in connection with the trade or business.

children.label: vii
children.text: Any item of deduction or loss properly allocable to an amount described in any of the preceding clauses.

children.heading: Treatment of reasonable compensation and guaranteed payments
children.label: 4 Treatment of reasonable compensation and guaranteed payments
A
children.text: reasonable compensation paid to the taxpayer by any qualified trade or business of the taxpayer for services rendered with respect to the trade or business,

children.label: B
children.text: any guaranteed payment described in section 707(c) paid to a partner for services rendered with respect to the trade or business,

children.label: C
children.text: to the extent provided in regulations, any payment described in section 707(a) to a partner for services rendered with respect to the trade or business, and

children.label: D
children.text: any amount with respect to which a deduction is allowable to the taxpayer under section 224(a) for the taxable year.

children.label: A
children.text: a specified service trade or business, or

children.label: B
children.text: the trade or business of performing services as an employee.

children.label: A
children.text: which is described in section 1202(e)(3)(A) (applied without regard to the words “engineering, architecture,”) or which would be so described if the term “employees or owners” were substituted for “employees” therein, or

children.label: B
children.text: which involves the performance of services that consist of investing and investment management, trading, or dealing in securities (as defined in section 475(c)(2)), partnership interests, or commodities (as defined in section 475(e)(2)).

children.heading: Exception for specified service businesses based on taxpayer’s income
children.label: 3 Exception for specified service businesses based on taxpayer’s income
i
children.text: any specified service trade or business of the taxpayer shall not fail to be treated as a qualified trade or business due to paragraph (1)(A), but

children.label: ii
children.text: only the applicable percentage of qualified items of income, gain, deduction, or loss, and the W–2 wages and the unadjusted basis immediately after acquisition of qualified property, of the taxpayer allocable to such specified service trade or business shall be taken into account in computing the qualified business income, W–2 wages, and the unadjusted basis immediately after acquisition of qualified property of the taxpayer for the taxable year for purposes of applying this section.

children.label: i
children.text: the taxable income of the taxpayer for the taxable year in excess of the threshold amount, bears to

children.label: ii
children.text: $75,000 ($150,000 in the case of a joint return).

children.heading: Taxable income
children.label: 1 Taxable income
children.text: Except as otherwise provided in subsection (g)(2)(B), taxable income shall be computed without regard to section 68 and without regard to any deduction allowable under this section.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The term “threshold amount” means $157,500 (200 percent of such amount in the case of a joint return).

children.label: i
children.text: such dollar amount, multiplied by

children.label: ii
children.text: the cost-of-living adjustment determined under section 1(f)(3) for the calendar year in which the taxable year begins, determined by substituting “calendar year 2017” for “calendar year 2016” in subparagraph (A)(ii) thereof.

children.label: A
children.text: is not a capital gain dividend, as defined in section 857(b)(3), and

children.label: B
children.text: is not qualified dividend income, as defined in section 1(h)(11).

children.label: A
children.text: the net amount of such taxpayer’s allocable share of each qualified item of income, gain, deduction, and loss (as defined in subsection (c)(3) and determined after the application of subsection (c)(4)) from a publicly traded partnership (as defined in section 7704(a)) which is not treated as a corporation under section 7704(c), plus2So in original. Probably should be “7704(b))”.2

children.label: B
children.text: any gain recognized by such taxpayer upon disposition of its interest in such partnership to the extent such gain is treated as an amount realized from the sale or exchange of property other than a capital asset under section 751(a).

children.heading: Application to partnerships and S corporations
children.label: 1 Application to partnerships and S corporations
i
children.text: this section shall be applied at the partner or shareholder level,

children.label: ii
children.text: each partner or shareholder shall take into account such person’s allocable share of each qualified item of income, gain, deduction, and loss, and

children.label: iii
children.text: each partner or shareholder shall be treated for purposes of subsection (b) as having W–2 wages and unadjusted basis immediately after acquisition of qualified property for the taxable year in an amount equal to such person’s allocable share of the W–2 wages and the unadjusted basis immediately after acquisition of qualified property of the partnership or S corporation for the taxable year (as determined under regulations prescribed by the Secretary).

children.heading: Application to trusts and estates
children.label: B Application to trusts and estates
children.text: Rules similar to the rules under section 199(d)(1)(B)(i) (as in effect on ) for the apportionment of W–2 wages shall apply to the apportionment of W–2 wages and the apportionment of unadjusted basis immediately after acquisition of qualified property under this section.December 1, 2017

children.heading: Treatment of trades or business in Puerto Rico
children.label: C Treatment of trades or business in Puerto Rico

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: In the case of any taxpayer with qualified business income from sources within the commonwealth of Puerto Rico, if all such income is taxable under section 1 for such taxable year, then for purposes of determining the qualified business income of such taxpayer for such taxable year, the term “United States” shall include the Commonwealth of Puerto Rico.

children.heading: Special rule for applying limit
children.label: ii Special rule for applying limit
children.text: In the case of any taxpayer described in clause (i), the determination of W–2 wages of such taxpayer with respect to any qualified trade or business conducted in Puerto Rico shall be made without regard to any exclusion under section 3401(a)(8) for remuneration paid for services in Puerto Rico.

children.heading: Coordination with minimum tax
children.label: 2 Coordination with minimum tax
children.text: For purposes of determining alternative minimum taxable income under section 55, qualified business income shall be determined without regard to any adjustments under sections 56 through 59.

children.heading: Deduction limited to income taxes
children.label: 3 Deduction limited to income taxes
children.text: The deduction under subsection (a) shall only be allowed for purposes of this chapter.

children.label: A
children.text: for requiring or restricting the allocation of items and wages under this section and such reporting requirements as the Secretary determines appropriate, and

children.label: B
children.text: for the application of this section in the case of tiered entities.

children.heading: Deduction for income attributable to domestic production activities of specified agricultural or horticultural cooperatives
children.label: g Deduction for income attributable to domestic production activities of specified agricultural or horticultural cooperatives
i
children.text: the qualified production activities income of the taxpayer for the taxable year, or

children.label: ii
children.text: the taxable income of the taxpayer for the taxable year.

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: The deduction allowable under subparagraph (A) for any taxable year shall not exceed 50 percent of the W–2 wages of the taxpayer for the taxable year.

children.heading: W–2 wages
children.label: ii W–2 wages
children.text: For purposes of this subparagraph, the W–2 wages of the taxpayer shall be determined in the same manner as under subsection (b)(4) (without regard to subparagraph (B) thereof and after application of subsection (b)(5)), except that such wages shall not include any amount which is not properly allocable to domestic production gross receipts for purposes of paragraph (3)(A).

children.heading: Taxable income of cooperatives determined without regard to certain deductions
children.label: C Taxable income of cooperatives determined without regard to certain deductions
children.text: For purposes of this subsection, the taxable income of a specified agricultural or horticultural cooperative shall be computed without regard to any deduction allowable under subsection (b) or (c) of section 1382 (relating to patronage dividends, per-unit retain allocations, and nonpatronage distributions).

children.label: i
children.text: allowed with respect to the portion of the qualified production activities income to which such payment is attributable, and

children.label: ii
children.text: identified by such cooperative in a written notice mailed to such taxpayer during the payment period described in section 1382(d).

children.heading: Limitation based on taxable income
children.label: B Limitation based on taxable income
children.text: The deduction allowed to any taxpayer under this paragraph shall not exceed the taxable income of the taxpayer determined without regard to section 68 or the deduction allowed under this paragraph and after taking into account any deduction allowed to the taxpayer under subsection (a) for the taxable year.

children.heading: Cooperative denied deduction for portion of qualified payments
children.label: C Cooperative denied deduction for portion of qualified payments
children.text: The taxable income of a specified agricultural or horticultural cooperative shall not be reduced under section 1382 by reason of that portion of any qualified payment as does not exceed the deduction allowable under subparagraph (A) with respect to such payment.

children.label: i
children.text: a taxpayer other than a corporation, or

children.label: ii
children.text: a specified agricultural or horticultural cooperative.

children.label: i
children.text: is described in paragraph (1) or (3) of section 1385(a),

children.label: ii
children.text: is received by such taxpayer from a specified agricultural or horticultural cooperative, and

children.label: iii
children.text: is attributable to qualified production activities income with respect to which a deduction is allowed to such cooperative under paragraph (1).

children.label: i
children.text: the taxpayer’s domestic production gross receipts for such taxable year, over

children.label: I
children.text: the cost of goods sold that are allocable to such receipts, and

children.label: II
children.text: other expenses, losses, or deductions (other than the deduction allowed under this subsection), which are properly allocable to such receipts.

children.heading: Allocation method
children.label: B Allocation method
children.text: The Secretary shall prescribe rules for the proper allocation of items described in subparagraph (A) for purposes of determining qualified production activities income. Such rules shall provide for the proper allocation of items whether or not such items are directly allocable to domestic production gross receipts.

children.heading: Special rules for determining costs
children.label: C Special rules for determining costs

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: For purposes of determining costs under subclause (I) of subparagraph (A)(ii), any item or service brought into the United States shall be treated as acquired by purchase, and its cost shall be treated as not less than its value immediately after it entered the United States. A similar rule shall apply in determining the adjusted basis of leased or rented property where the lease or rental gives rise to domestic production gross receipts.

children.heading: Exports for further manufacture
children.label: ii Exports for further manufacture
children.text: In the case of any property described in clause (i) that had been exported by the taxpayer for further manufacture, the increase in cost or adjusted basis under clause (i) shall not exceed the difference between the value of the property when exported and the value of the property when brought back into the United States after the further manufacture.

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: The term “domestic production gross receipts” means the gross receipts of the taxpayer which are derived from any lease, rental, license, sale, exchange, or other disposition of any agricultural or horticultural product which was manufactured, produced, grown, or extracted by the taxpayer (determined after the application of paragraph (4)(B)) in whole or significant part within the United States. Such term shall not include gross receipts of the taxpayer which are derived from the lease, rental, license, sale, exchange, or other disposition of land.

children.heading: In general
children.label: I In general
children.text: The term “domestic production gross receipts” shall not include any gross receipts of the taxpayer derived from property leased, licensed, or rented by the taxpayer for use by any related person.

children.heading: Related person
children.label: II Related person
children.text: For purposes of subclause (I), a person shall be treated as related to another person if such persons are treated as a single employer under subsection (a) or (b) of section 52 or subsection (m) or () of section 414, except that determinations under subsections (a) and (b) of section 52 shall be made without regard to section 1563(b).o

children.label: i
children.text: in the manufacturing, production, growth, or extraction in whole or significant part of any agricultural or horticultural product, or

children.label: ii
children.text: in the marketing of agricultural or horticultural products.

children.heading: Application to marketing cooperatives
children.label: B Application to marketing cooperatives
children.text: A specified agricultural or horticultural cooperative described in subparagraph (A)(ii) shall be treated as having manufactured, produced, grown, or extracted in whole or significant part any agricultural or horticultural product marketed by the specified agricultural or horticultural cooperative which its patrons have so manufactured, produced, grown, or extracted.

children.heading: Special rule for affiliated groups
children.label: A Special rule for affiliated groups

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: All members of an expanded affiliated group shall be treated as a single corporation for purposes of this subsection.

children.heading: Partnerships owned by expanded affiliated groups
children.label: ii Partnerships owned by expanded affiliated groups
children.text: For purposes of paragraph (3)(D), if all of the interests in the capital and profits of a partnership are owned by members of a single expanded affiliated group at all times during the taxable year of such partnership, the partnership and all members of such group shall be treated as a single taxpayer during such period.

children.label: I
children.text: by substituting “more than 50 percent” for “at least 80 percent” each place it appears, and

children.label: II
children.text: without regard to paragraphs (2) and (4) of section 1504(b).

children.heading: Allocation of deduction
children.label: iv Allocation of deduction
children.text: Except as provided in regulations, the deduction under paragraph (1) shall be allocated among the members of the expanded affiliated group in proportion to each member’s respective amount (if any) of qualified production activities income.

children.heading: Special rule for cooperative partners
children.label: B Special rule for cooperative partners
children.text: In the case of a specified agricultural or horticultural cooperative which is a partner in a partnership, rules similar to the rules of subsection (f)(1) shall apply for purposes of this subsection.

children.heading: Trade or business requirement
children.label: C Trade or business requirement
children.text: This subsection shall be applied by only taking into account items which are attributable to the actual conduct of a trade or business.

children.heading: Unrelated business taxable income
children.label: D Unrelated business taxable income
children.text: For purposes of determining the tax imposed by section 511, this section shall be applied by substituting “unrelated business taxable income” for “taxable income” each place it appears in this section (other than this subparagraph).

children.heading: Special rule for cooperative with oil related qualified production activities income
children.label: E Special rule for cooperative with oil related qualified production activities income
I
children.text: the oil related qualified production activities income of the cooperative for the taxable year,

children.label: II
children.text: the qualified production activities income of the cooperative for the taxable year, or

children.label: III
children.text: taxable income.

children.heading: Oil related qualified production activities income
children.label: ii Oil related qualified production activities income
children.text: For purposes of this subparagraph, the term “oil related qualified production activities income” means for any taxable year the qualified production activities income which is attributable to the production, refining, processing, transportation, or distribution of oil, gas, or any primary product thereof (within the meaning of section 927(a)(2)(C), as in effect before its repeal) during such taxable year.

children.heading: Regulations
children.label: 6 Regulations
children.text: The Secretary shall prescribe such regulations as are necessary to carry out the purposes of this subsection, including regulations which prevent more than 1 taxpayer from being allowed a deduction under this subsection with respect to any activity described in paragraph (3)(D)(i). Such regulations shall be based on the regulations applicable to cooperatives and their patrons under section 199 (as in effect before its repeal).
apply rules similar to the rules under section 179(d)(2) in order to prevent the manipulation of the depreciable period of qualified property using transactions between related parties, and
prescribe rules for determining the unadjusted basis immediately after acquisition of qualified property acquired in like-kind exchanges or involuntary conversions.

children.heading: Minimum deduction for active qualified business income
children.label: i Minimum deduction for active qualified business income
A
children.text: the amount of such deduction determined without regard to this subsection, or

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The term “applicable taxpayer” means, with respect to any taxable year, a taxpayer whose aggregate qualified business income with respect to all active qualified trades or businesses of the taxpayer for such taxable year is at least $1,000.

children.heading: Active qualified trade or business
children.label: B Active qualified trade or business
children.text: The term “active qualified trade or business” means, with respect to any taxpayer for any taxable year, any qualified trade or business of the taxpayer in which the taxpayer materially participates (within the meaning of section 469(h)).

children.label: A
children.text: such dollar amount, multiplied by

children.label: B
children.text: the cost-of-living adjustment determined under section 1(f)(3) for the calendar year in which the taxable year begins, determined by substituting “calendar year 2025” for “calendar year 2016” in subparagraph (A)(ii) thereof.
